title: The Man Who Watches Trains
text_zh: 火车站旁边有一个小饭馆。每天下午，一个男人坐在门口，看火车从左边到右边，又从右边到左边。饭馆的人问他：「你为什么每天都来？」他笑了笑，说：「因为火车知道去哪里，我不知道。」
text_pinyin: Huǒchēzhàn pángbiān yǒu yí gè xiǎo fànguǎn. Měi tiān xiàwǔ, yí gè nánrén zuò zài ménkǒu, kàn huǒchē cóng zuǒbiān dào yòubiān, yòu cóng yòubiān dào zuǒbiān. Fànguǎn de rén wèn tā:「Nǐ wèishénme měi tiān dōu lái?」Tā xiào le xiào, shuō:「Yīnwèi huǒchē zhīdào qù nǎlǐ, wǒ bù zhīdào.」
text_en: Next to the train station there's a small restaurant. Every afternoon, a man sits at the entrance watching trains go from left to right, then right to left. The restaurant people ask him: 'Why do you come every day?' He smiles and says: 'Because the trains know where they're going. I don't.'

questions.q_zh: 男人每天在哪里看火车？
questions.q_en: Where does the man watch trains every day?
Why does the man come every day?

options.text_en: because he works at the restaurant
because he wants to take the train

options.text: 因为火车知道去哪里，他不知道
options.pinyin: yīnwèi huǒchē zhīdào qù nǎlǐ, tā bù zhīdào
options.text_en: because trains know where to go, he doesn't

questions.q_zh: 男人说话的时候，他怎么样？
questions.q_en: How is the man when he speaks?

title: Afternoon in the Teahouse
text_zh: 这个茶馆很旧了，但是每天都有人来。有的老人从早上坐到下午，从来不说话。他们喝茶，看报纸，有时候看看窗外面。外面的路上，人走得很快。里面的人走得很慢。我觉得这里的时间和外面的不一样。
text_pinyin: Zhège cháguǎn hěn jiù le, dànshì měi tiān dōu yǒu rén lái. Yǒude lǎorén cóng zǎoshang zuò dào xiàwǔ, cónglái bù shuōhuà. Tāmen hē chá, kàn bàozhǐ, yǒu shíhou kànkan chuāng wàimiàn. Wàimiàn de lù shàng, rén zǒu de hěn kuài. Lǐmiàn de rén zǒu de hěn màn. Wǒ juéde zhèlǐ de shíjiān hé wàimiàn de bù yíyàng.
text_en: This teahouse is old, but people come every day. Some old people sit from morning until afternoon without ever speaking. They drink tea, read newspapers, sometimes look out the window. On the road outside, people walk fast. Inside, people move slowly. I feel the time here is different from outside.

questions.q_en: What do the old people do in the teahouse?

questions.q_zh: 茶馆里面和外面有什么不一样？
questions.q_en: What's different between inside and outside the teahouse?

options.pinyin: lǐmiàn hěn rè, wàimiàn hěn lěng
options.text_en: inside is hot, outside is cold
inside is slow, outside is fast
inside is big, outside is small
inside is new, outside is old

questions.q_zh: 这个茶馆是新的还是旧的？
questions.q_en: Is this teahouse new or old?

title: The Cat on the Roof
text_zh: 我房间的窗外面，有一只黑猫。它每天早上都在对面的房顶上走。从左走到右，又从右走到左。有时候它停下来，看下面的路。我觉得它也在看我。我们每天早上都这样，我看它，它看我。
text_pinyin: Wǒ fángjiān de chuāng wàimiàn, yǒu yì zhī hēi māo. Tā měi tiān zǎoshang dōu zài duìmiàn de fángdǐng shàng zǒu. Cóng zuǒ zǒu dào yòu, yòu cóng yòu zǒu dào zuǒ. Yǒu shíhou tā tíng xiàlái, kàn xiàmiàn de lù. Wǒ juéde tā yě zài kàn wǒ. Wǒmen měi tiān zǎoshang dōu zhèyàng, wǒ kàn tā, tā kàn wǒ.
text_en: Outside my room window, there's a black cat. Every morning it walks on the rooftop across the way. Left to right, then right to left. Sometimes it stops and looks at the road below. I think it's also watching me. Every morning we're like this — I watch it, it watches me.

questions.q_en: What color is the cat?
Where does the cat walk every day?
What do 'I' and the cat do every morning?

title: A Bench by the Lake
text_zh: 公园里有一个很长的椅子，在湖旁边。白天，很多人坐在那里。但是晚上七点以后，只有一个女人来。她不看手机，不看书，只是坐着看水。她每天都穿白色的衣服。我很想知道她在想什么，但是我从来没有问过。
text_pinyin: Gōngyuán lǐ yǒu yí gè hěn cháng de yǐzi, zài hú pángbiān. Báitiān, hěn duō rén zuò zài nàlǐ. Dànshì wǎnshang qī diǎn yǐhòu, zhǐ yǒu yí gè nǚrén lái. Tā bú kàn shǒujī, bú kàn shū, zhǐ shì zuòzhe kàn shuǐ. Tā měi tiān dōu chuān báisè de yīfu. Wǒ hěn xiǎng zhīdào tā zài xiǎng shénme, dànshì wǒ cónglái méiyǒu wèn guò.
text_en: In the park there's a long bench by the lake. During the day, many people sit there. But after seven in the evening, only one woman comes. She doesn't look at her phone or read — she just sits watching the water. She always wears white clothes. I want to know what she's thinking, but I've never asked.

questions.q_en: What does the woman do at night?

questions.q_zh: 女人穿什么颜色的衣服？
questions.q_en: What color clothes does the woman wear?
Has 'I' ever asked the woman?

title: Two Cups
text_zh: 咖啡店里，一个女人面前有两个杯子。一个是她的，一个是谁的？对面的椅子上没有人。她有时候看看那个杯子，有时候看看门。咖啡慢慢地冷了。她等了很长时间，最后一个人也没有来。她站起来的时候，没有拿走那个杯子。
text_pinyin: Kāfēidiàn lǐ, yí gè nǚrén miànqián yǒu liǎng gè bēizi. Yí gè shì tā de, yí gè shì shéi de? Duìmiàn de yǐzi shàng méiyǒu rén. Tā yǒu shíhou kànkan nàge bēizi, yǒu shíhou kànkan mén. Kāfēi mànmàn de lěng le. Tā děng le hěn cháng shíjiān, zuìhòu yí gè rén yě méiyǒu lái. Tā zhàn qǐlái de shíhou, méiyǒu ná zǒu nàge bēizi.
text_en: In a coffee shop, a woman has two cups in front of her. One is hers — whose is the other? The chair across from her is empty. Sometimes she looks at that cup, sometimes at the door. The coffee slowly grows cold. She waits a long time. In the end, nobody comes. When she stands up, she doesn't take that cup.

questions.q_en: Is anyone in the chair across from her?
What is the woman waiting for?

questions.q_zh: 最后她走的时候怎么样？
questions.q_en: What happens when she finally leaves?

options.pinyin: tā ná zǒu le liǎng gè bēizi
tā méiyǒu ná zǒu nàge bēizi
tā yòu yào le yí gè bēizi

title: The Shoe Repairman
text_zh: 路旁边有一个老人，他帮别人修鞋。他的手很快，但是他从来不说话。人们把鞋给他，他看一看，就开始做了。做完了，他告诉你多少钱。虽然他不笑，但是每双鞋他都修得很好。我觉得他是用手说话的人。
text_pinyin: Lù pángbiān yǒu yí gè lǎorén, tā bāng biéren xiū xié. Tā de shǒu hěn kuài, dànshì tā cónglái bù shuōhuà. Rénmen bǎ xié gěi tā, tā kàn yi kàn, jiù kāishǐ zuò le. Zuò wán le, tā gàosu nǐ duōshao qián. Suīrán tā bú xiào, dànshì měi shuāng xié tā dōu xiū de hěn hǎo. Wǒ juéde tā shì yòng shǒu shuōhuà de rén.
text_en: By the road there's an old man who repairs shoes for people. His hands are quick, but he never speaks. People give him their shoes, he takes a look, then starts working. When he's done, he tells you the price. Although he doesn't smile, every pair of shoes he fixes well. I think he's someone who speaks with his hands.

questions.q_en: What does the old man do?
Although the old man doesn't smile, ...
What does 'I' think about the old man?

options.pinyin: tā shì yòng shǒu shuōhuà de rén

title: Night Bus
text_zh: 晚上十点的公共汽车上，人很少。一个女人靠着窗户睡着了，手里还拿着手机。一个学生在看书，但是眼睛快要闭上了。司机开得很慢，好像他也不想快到。车外面的路灯一个一个过去了。这是一天里最安静的时候。
text_pinyin: Wǎnshang shí diǎn de gōnggòng qìchē shàng, rén hěn shǎo. Yí gè nǚrén kàozhe chuānghu shuìzháo le, shǒu lǐ hái názhe shǒujī. Yí gè xuéshēng zài kàn shū, dànshì yǎnjing kuài yào bì shàng le. Sījī kāi de hěn màn, hǎoxiàng tā yě bù xiǎng kuài dào. Chē wàimiàn de lùdēng yí gè yí gè guòqù le. Zhè shì yì tiān lǐ zuì ānjìng de shíhou.
text_en: On the ten o'clock bus, there are few people. A woman has fallen asleep leaning against the window, still holding her phone. A student is reading, but his eyes are about to close. The driver goes slowly, as if he doesn't want to arrive quickly either. The streetlights outside pass by one by one. This is the quietest moment of the day.

questions.q_en: Are there many people on the bus?
What is the woman holding?
How does the driver drive?

title: The Sound of Rain on Leaves
text_zh: 今天下午又下雨了。我坐在房间里，窗户开着。外面有一棵大树，雨打在树上，声音很好听。比音乐还好听。楼下有人快跑过去了，但是我不想动。有时候，什么都不做也很好。就这样听着雨，从三点到五点。
text_pinyin: Jīntiān xiàwǔ yòu xià yǔ le. Wǒ zuò zài fángjiān lǐ, chuānghu kāizhe. Wàimiàn yǒu yì kē dà shù, yǔ dǎ zài shù shàng, shēngyīn hěn hǎotīng. Bǐ yīnyuè hái hǎotīng. Lóu xià yǒu rén kuài pǎo guòqù le, dànshì wǒ bù xiǎng dòng. Yǒu shíhou, shénme dōu bú zuò yě hěn hǎo. Jiù zhèyàng tīngzhe yǔ, cóng sān diǎn dào wǔ diǎn.
text_en: It rained again this afternoon. I sit in my room with the window open. Outside there's a big tree; the rain hitting the leaves sounds beautiful. More beautiful than music. Downstairs someone runs past quickly, but I don't want to move. Sometimes doing nothing at all is fine. Just listening to rain like this, from three to five.

questions.q_en: How does 'I' feel about the sound of rain?
How long did 'I' listen to the rain?
What is someone doing downstairs?

title: Two Sides of One Street
text_zh: 这条路很有意思。左边都是新的商店，很贵，里面的人穿得很好。右边都是旧的小店，很便宜，门口坐着老人。从左边走到右边，只要十步，但是好像从一个地方到了另一个地方。我喜欢在右边吃面条，然后去左边买咖啡。
text_pinyin: Zhè tiáo lù hěn yǒu yìsi. Zuǒbiān dōu shì xīn de shāngdiàn, hěn guì, lǐmiàn de rén chuān de hěn hǎo. Yòubiān dōu shì jiù de xiǎodiàn, hěn piányi, ménkǒu zuòzhe lǎorén. Cóng zuǒbiān zǒu dào yòubiān, zhǐ yào shí bù, dànshì hǎoxiàng cóng yí gè dìfang dào le lìng yí gè dìfang. Wǒ xǐhuan zài yòubiān chī miàntiáo, ránhòu qù zuǒbiān mǎi kāfēi.
text_en: This street is interesting. The left side is all new shops, expensive, people inside dressed well. The right side is all old little shops, cheap, old people sitting in doorways. From left to right is only ten steps, but it feels like going from one world to another. I like eating noodles on the right side, then going to the left for coffee.

questions.q_zh: 路的左边和右边有什么不一样？
questions.q_en: What's different about the left and right sides?

options.pinyin: zuǒbiān xīn, guì; yòubiān jiù, piányi
options.text_en: left is new, expensive; right is old, cheap
left is far, right is near

options.text: 左边有学校，右边有医院
options.pinyin: zuǒbiān yǒu xuéxiào, yòubiān yǒu yīyuàn
options.text_en: left has school, right has hospital

questions.q_zh: 从左边到右边要走多远？
questions.q_en: How far is it from left to right?
Which side does 'I' eat noodles on?

title: The Overnight Bookstore
text_zh: 我家旁边有一个书店，它从来不关门。晚上十二点去，还有人在里面看书。有的人坐在地上，有的人靠着书架睡着了。店里的灯不太亮，很暖。我有时候晚上睡不着，就去那里坐一坐。不买书也没关系。这个书店让我觉得，这个城市还是有一些好的地方。
text_pinyin: Wǒ jiā pángbiān yǒu yí gè shūdiàn, tā cónglái bù guān mén. Wǎnshang shí'èr diǎn qù, hái yǒu rén zài lǐmiàn kàn shū. Yǒude rén zuò zài dì shàng, yǒude rén kàozhe shūjià shuìzháo le. Diàn lǐ de dēng bú tài liàng, hěn nuǎn. Wǒ yǒu shíhou wǎnshang shuì bu zháo, jiù qù nàlǐ zuò yi zuò. Bù mǎi shū yě méi guānxi. Zhège shūdiàn ràng wǒ juéde, zhège chéngshì háishi yǒu yìxiē hǎo de dìfang.
text_en: Near my home there's a bookstore that never closes. Go at midnight and people are still reading inside. Some sit on the floor, some have fallen asleep leaning against shelves. The lights are dim and warm. Sometimes when I can't sleep, I go sit there for a bit. It's fine not to buy anything. This bookstore makes me feel the city still has some good places.

questions.q_zh: 这个书店什么时候关门？
questions.q_en: When does the bookstore close?
Why does 'I' go to the bookstore?

options.text: 睡不着的时候去坐一坐
options.pinyin: shuì bu zháo de shíhou qù zuò yi zuò
options.text_en: to sit for a bit when can't sleep

questions.q_en: How does the bookstore make 'I' feel?

options.pinyin: chéngshì hái yǒu hǎo de dìfang
options.text_en: the city still has good places

title: The Fruit Seller's Corner
text_zh: 每天早上，一个女人在路口卖水果。西瓜、苹果，还有别的。她的声音很大：「便宜！便宜！今天的西瓜很好！」旁边的人都认识她了。下雨的时候她也在，很热的时候也在。有一天她没来，旁边卖报纸的人说：「今天觉得少了什么。」
text_pinyin: Měi tiān zǎoshang, yí gè nǚrén zài lùkǒu mài shuǐguǒ. Xīguā, píngguǒ, hái yǒu bié de. Tā de shēngyīn hěn dà:「Piányi! Piányi! Jīntiān de xīguā hěn hǎo!」Pángbiān de rén dōu rènshi tā le. Xià yǔ de shíhou tā yě zài, hěn rè de shíhou yě zài. Yǒu yì tiān tā méi lái, pángbiān mài bàozhǐ de rén shuō:「Jīntiān juéde shǎo le shénme.」
text_en: Every morning, a woman sells fruit at the corner. Watermelons, apples, and more. Her voice is loud: 'Cheap! Cheap! Today's watermelons are good!' Everyone nearby knows her. She's there when it rains, there when it's hot. One day she didn't come, and the newspaper seller next to her said: 'Today it feels like something's missing.'

questions.q_en: What does the woman sell?
Is she there when it rains?

questions.q_zh: 她没来的那天，卖报纸的人觉得怎么样？
questions.q_en: How did the newspaper seller feel the day she didn't come?

title: Upstairs, Downstairs
text_zh: 我住在三楼。楼上四楼住着一个人，每天晚上很晚回来。我听得到他开门、走路、洗东西的声音。楼下二楼的人很早起来，每天早上六点就开始做饭。我闻得到。虽然我们从来没见过面，但是我知道他们什么时候起来，什么时候睡觉。住在一起的人，不一定认识。
text_pinyin: Wǒ zhù zài sān lóu. Lóu shàng sì lóu zhùzhe yí gè rén, měi tiān wǎnshang hěn wǎn huílái. Wǒ tīng de dào tā kāi mén, zǒulù, xǐ dōngxi de shēngyīn. Lóu xià èr lóu de rén hěn zǎo qǐlái, měi tiān zǎoshang liù diǎn jiù kāishǐ zuò fàn. Wǒ wén de dào. Suīrán wǒmen cónglái méi jiàn guò miàn, dànshì wǒ zhīdào tāmen shénme shíhou qǐlái, shénme shíhou shuìjiào. Zhù zài yìqǐ de rén, bù yídìng rènshi.
text_en: I live on the third floor. Upstairs on the fourth floor, someone comes back very late every night. I can hear them open the door, walk, wash things. The person below on the second floor wakes early — starts cooking at six every morning. I can smell it. Although we've never met, I know when they wake up and when they sleep. People who live together don't necessarily know each other.

questions.q_zh: 四楼的人什么时候回来？
questions.q_en: When does the person on the fourth floor come back?
How does 'I' know the second-floor person is cooking?
Have they met?

title: The Bike Lane at Rush Hour
text_zh: 早上八点，自行车路上有很多人。大家都骑得很快，从这边到公司去。有的人一只手骑车，一只手拿着早饭。有的人还在打电话。有一个人骑得很慢，后面的人都很着急。但是她不着急。她一边骑车，一边看路旁边的花。我想：也许她是对的。
text_pinyin: Zǎoshang bā diǎn, zìxíngchē lù shàng yǒu hěn duō rén. Dàjiā dōu qí de hěn kuài, cóng zhèbiān dào gōngsī qù. Yǒude rén yì zhī shǒu qí chē, yì zhī shǒu názhe zǎofàn. Yǒude rén hái zài dǎ diànhuà. Yǒu yí gè rén qí de hěn màn, hòumiàn de rén dōu hěn zhāojí. Dànshì tā bù zhāojí. Tā yìbiān qí chē, yìbiān kàn lù pángbiān de huā. Wǒ xiǎng: yěxǔ tā shì duì de.
text_en: At eight in the morning, the bike lane is packed. Everyone rides fast, heading to their companies. Some ride with one hand, holding breakfast in the other. Some are on the phone. One person rides very slowly — everyone behind is anxious. But she isn't. She rides while looking at the flowers by the road. I think: maybe she's the one who's right.

questions.q_en: Why is everyone riding fast?
What is the slow rider doing?

options.text_en: looking at flowers by the road

questions.q_en: What does 'I' think about the slow rider?

title: The Hospital Waiting Room
text_zh: 医院的等候室里，大家都在等。有的人看手机，有的人看报纸，有的人什么都不做，只是坐着。一个妈妈让她的孩子别哭，但是孩子还是哭。一个老人闭着眼睛，我不知道他是在睡觉还是在想事情。这里每个人都有一个问题，但是没有人说出来。大家只是等。
text_pinyin: Yīyuàn de děnghòushì lǐ, dàjiā dōu zài děng. Yǒude rén kàn shǒujī, yǒude rén kàn bàozhǐ, yǒude rén shénme dōu bú zuò, zhǐ shì zuòzhe. Yí gè māma ràng tā de háizi bié kū, dànshì háizi háishi kū. Yí gè lǎorén bìzhe yǎnjing, wǒ bù zhīdào tā shì zài shuìjiào háishi zài xiǎng shìqing. Zhèlǐ měi gè rén dōu yǒu yí gè wèntí, dànshì méiyǒu rén shuō chūlái. Dàjiā zhǐ shì děng.
text_en: In the hospital waiting room, everyone waits. Some look at phones, some read newspapers, some do nothing — just sit. A mother tells her child not to cry, but the child still cries. An old man has his eyes closed; I don't know if he's sleeping or thinking. Everyone here has a problem, but nobody says it aloud. They just wait.

questions.q_en: What does the mother tell the child to do?
What is the old man doing?
How are the people in the waiting room?

options.text: 每个人都有问题，但没人说出来
options.pinyin: měi gè rén dōu yǒu wèntí, dàn méi rén shuō chūlái
options.text_en: everyone has a problem but nobody says it

title: The School After Hours
text_zh: 下午四点，学生们都走了。教室里只有桌子和椅子。黑板上还有老师写的字。地上有一支笔，不知道是谁的。窗户还开着，风吹进来。白天这里有很多声音，现在什么声音都没有了。学校在白天和晚上是两个不一样的地方。
text_pinyin: Xiàwǔ sì diǎn, xuéshēngmen dōu zǒu le. Jiàoshì lǐ zhǐ yǒu zhuōzi hé yǐzi. Hēibǎn shàng hái yǒu lǎoshī xiě de zì. Dì shàng yǒu yì zhī bǐ, bù zhīdào shì shéi de. Chuānghu hái kāizhe, fēng chuī jìnlái. Báitiān zhèlǐ yǒu hěn duō shēngyīn, xiànzài shénme shēngyīn dōu méiyǒu le. Xuéxiào zài báitiān hé wǎnshang shì liǎng gè bù yíyàng de dìfang.
text_en: At four in the afternoon, all the students have left. The classroom has only desks and chairs. On the blackboard, the teacher's writing remains. On the floor there's a pen — no one knows whose. The window is still open; wind blows in. During the day this place is full of sounds. Now there's nothing. School during the day and at night are two different places.

questions.q_en: When did the students leave?
What's on the floor?
What is the classroom like now?

title: The Post Office Queue
text_zh: 邮局里只有两个窗口，但是有二十个人在等。大家都站着，一个在另一个后面。前面的人很慢，后面的人很着急。一个老人要寄东西，他准备了很多纸，但是写错了。工作的人让他再写一次。后面的人都看着他，但是没有人帮他。我帮他写完了。他对我笑了笑，说了一声谢谢。
text_pinyin: Yóujú lǐ zhǐ yǒu liǎng gè chuāngkǒu, dànshì yǒu èrshí gè rén zài děng. Dàjiā dōu zhànzhe, yí gè zài lìng yí gè hòumiàn. Qiánmiàn de rén hěn màn, hòumiàn de rén hěn zhāojí. Yí gè lǎorén yào jì dōngxi, tā zhǔnbèi le hěn duō zhǐ, dànshì xiě cuò le. Gōngzuò de rén ràng tā zài xiě yí cì. Hòumiàn de rén dōu kànzhe tā, dànshì méiyǒu rén bāng tā. Wǒ bāng tā xiě wán le. Tā duì wǒ xiào le xiào, shuō le yì shēng xièxie.
text_en: The post office has only two windows, but twenty people are waiting. Everyone stands in line, one behind another. The people in front are slow; the people behind are anxious. An old man wants to mail something. He prepared lots of papers but wrote it wrong. The worker tells him to write it again. Everyone behind watches him, but nobody helps. I help him finish writing. He smiles at me and says thank you.

questions.q_en: How many windows does the post office have?
What is the old man's problem?
Who helped the old man?

title: The Wrong Ticket
text_zh: 我在火车上找到了我的座位，但是上面坐着一个男人。我给他看我的票，他也给我看他的票。一样的座位号。我们都觉得很奇怪。我又看了一下我的票——日期是对的，时间是对的，但是火车号不对。这不是我的火车。我的火车已经走了。
text_pinyin: Wǒ zài huǒchē shàng zhǎo dào le wǒ de zuòwèi, dànshì shàngmiàn zuòzhe yí gè nánrén. Wǒ gěi tā kàn wǒ de piào, tā yě gěi wǒ kàn tā de piào. Yíyàng de zuòwèi hào. Wǒmen dōu juéde hěn qíguài. Wǒ yòu kàn le yíxià wǒ de piào——rìqī shì duì de, shíjiān shì duì de, dànshì huǒchē hào búduì. Zhè búshì wǒ de huǒchē. Wǒ de huǒchē yǐjīng zǒu le.
text_en: I find my seat on the train, but a man is sitting there. I show him my ticket; he shows me his. Same seat number. We're both confused. I look at my ticket again — the date is right, the time is right, but the train number is wrong. This isn't my train. My train has already left.

questions.q_zh: 为什么两个人的座位号一样？
questions.q_en: Why do both people have the same seat number?

options.text_en: the man sat in the wrong seat
the station sold a wrong ticket

questions.q_en: What's wrong on 'my' ticket?
What happened to 'my' train?

title: The Extra Umbrella
text_zh: 下雨天，我去咖啡店。进门的时候，门口有三把伞。我把我的伞也放在那里。喝完咖啡以后，我去拿伞。但是现在有五把伞了。我只带了一把来，店里的人说他们也只带了一把。那多出来的那把是谁的？我看了看外面——雨已经停了。也许那把伞不想走了。
text_pinyin: Xià yǔ tiān, wǒ qù kāfēidiàn. Jìn mén de shíhou, ménkǒu yǒu sān bǎ sǎn. Wǒ bǎ wǒ de sǎn yě fàng zài nàlǐ. Hē wán kāfēi yǐhòu, wǒ qù ná sǎn. Dànshì xiànzài yǒu wǔ bǎ sǎn le. Wǒ zhǐ dài le yì bǎ lái, diàn lǐ de rén shuō tāmen yě zhǐ dài le yì bǎ. Nà duō chūlái de nà bǎ shì shéi de? Wǒ kàn le kàn wàimiàn——yǔ yǐjīng tíng le. Yěxǔ nà bǎ sǎn bù xiǎng zǒu le.
text_en: On a rainy day, I go to a coffee shop. When I enter, there are three umbrellas at the door. I put mine there too. After finishing my coffee, I go to get my umbrella. But now there are five. I only brought one; the people in the shop say they also only brought one each. Whose is the extra one? I look outside — the rain has stopped. Maybe that umbrella doesn't want to leave.

questions.q_en: How many umbrellas were there when 'I' entered?
How many umbrellas were there later?
What's it like outside when 'I' comes out?

title: The Phone Call at Midnight
text_zh: 昨天晚上十二点，我的手机响了。我看了一下——是一个我不认识的号码。我接了。对面的人说：「你好，我找王先生。」我说：「你打错了，我不是王先生。」他说：「对不起。」然后就挂了。但是今天早上，我又接到了一样的电话，一样的声音，一样的话。他又说：「你好，我找王先生。」我说：「你昨天也打过。」他没说话，就挂了。
text_pinyin: Zuótiān wǎnshang shí'èr diǎn, wǒ de shǒujī xiǎng le. Wǒ kàn le yíxià——shì yí gè wǒ bú rènshi de hàomǎ. Wǒ jiē le. Duìmiàn de rén shuō:「Nǐ hǎo, wǒ zhǎo Wáng xiānsheng.」Wǒ shuō:「Nǐ dǎ cuò le, wǒ búshì Wáng xiānsheng.」Tā shuō:「Duìbuqǐ.」Ránhòu jiù guà le. Dànshì jīntiān zǎoshang, wǒ yòu jiē dào le yíyàng de diànhuà, yíyàng de shēngyīn, yíyàng de huà. Tā yòu shuō:「Nǐ hǎo, wǒ zhǎo Wáng xiānsheng.」Wǒ shuō:「Nǐ zuótiān yě dǎ guò.」Tā méi shuōhuà, jiù guà le.
text_en: Last night at midnight, my phone rang. I looked — an unknown number. I answered. The person said: 'Hello, I'm looking for Mr. Wang.' I said: 'Wrong number, I'm not Mr. Wang.' He said: 'Sorry.' Then hung up. But this morning, I got the same call. Same voice, same words. He said again: 'Hello, I'm looking for Mr. Wang.' I said: 'You called yesterday too.' He said nothing and hung up.

questions.q_en: Who is the caller looking for?

questions.q_zh: 第二次打电话是什么时候？
questions.q_en: When was the second call?

questions.q_zh: 「我」告诉他什么以后，他怎么样？
questions.q_en: What did the caller do after 'I' told him he called yesterday?

options.pinyin: tā méi shuōhuà jiù guà le
options.text_en: he said nothing and hung up

options.pinyin: tā yòu wèn le yí cì

title: Two Names
text_zh: 我有两个名字。在公司，大家叫我英文名字David。在家里，妈妈叫我中文名字大伟。有时候我觉得David和大伟不是一个人。David说英语，穿黑色衣服，走得很快。大伟说中文，喜欢慢慢吃妈妈做的面条。晚上回家，我从David变成大伟。这两个人，我都是。
text_pinyin: Wǒ yǒu liǎng gè míngzi. Zài gōngsī, dàjiā jiào wǒ yīngwén míngzi David. Zài jiā lǐ, māma jiào wǒ zhōngwén míngzi Dàwěi. Yǒu shíhou wǒ juéde David hé Dàwěi búshì yí gè rén. David shuō yīngyǔ, chuān hēisè yīfu, zǒu de hěn kuài. Dàwěi shuō zhōngwén, xǐhuan mànmàn chī māma zuò de miàntiáo. Wǎnshang huí jiā, wǒ cóng David biànchéng Dàwěi. Zhè liǎng gè rén, wǒ dōu shì.
text_en: I have two names. At the company, everyone calls me David. At home, Mom calls me Dawei. Sometimes I feel David and Dawei aren't the same person. David speaks English, wears black, walks fast. Dawei speaks Chinese, likes slowly eating Mom's noodles. At night when I go home, I change from David into Dawei. Both of them are me.

questions.q_en: What do people call him at work?
What does Dawei like to do?

options.pinyin: mànmàn chī māma zuò de miàntiáo

questions.q_en: How does he feel about David and Dawei?

options.text: 有时候觉得不是一个人
options.pinyin: yǒu shíhou juéde búshì yí gè rén
options.text_en: sometimes feels they're not the same person

title: The Accent
text_zh: 我在北京住了三年了。我说中文的时候，别人总是问我：「你是哪里人？」因为我的口音和北京人不一样。我来自一个很小的城市，那里的人说话很慢，声音很轻。在北京大家说话很快，声音很大。有时候我想让自己说得像北京人，但是我做不到。我朋友告诉我：「别变，你的口音很好听。」
text_pinyin: Wǒ zài Běijīng zhù le sān nián le. Wǒ shuō zhōngwén de shíhou, biéren zǒngshì wèn wǒ:「Nǐ shì nǎlǐ rén?」Yīnwèi wǒ de kǒuyīn hé Běijīng rén bù yíyàng. Wǒ láizì yí gè hěn xiǎo de chéngshì, nàlǐ de rén shuōhuà hěn màn, shēngyīn hěn qīng. Zài Běijīng dàjiā shuōhuà hěn kuài, shēngyīn hěn dà. Yǒu shíhou wǒ xiǎng ràng zìjǐ shuō de xiàng Běijīng rén, dànshì wǒ zuò bu dào. Wǒ péngyou gàosu wǒ:「Bié biàn, nǐ de kǒuyīn hěn hǎotīng.」
text_en: I've lived in Beijing for three years. When I speak Chinese, people always ask: 'Where are you from?' Because my accent is different from Beijing people's. I come from a very small city where people speak slowly and softly. In Beijing everyone speaks fast and loud. Sometimes I want to make myself sound like a Beijing person, but I can't. My friend tells me: 'Don't change — your accent sounds nice.'

questions.q_zh: 别人为什么总问他是哪里人？
questions.q_en: Why do people always ask where he's from?

options.pinyin: yīnwèi tā chuān de bù yíyàng
yīnwèi tā de kǒuyīn bù yíyàng
yīnwèi tā de liǎn bù yíyàng

questions.q_en: Where does he come from?

options.pinyin: yí gè hěn dà de chéngshì
yí gè hěn xiǎo de chéngshì

questions.q_en: What does his friend tell him?

options.text_en: don't change, your accent sounds nice

title: The Wrong Medicine
text_zh: 我生病了，医生给了我两种药。白色的早上吃，红色的晚上吃。但是我吃错了——早上吃了红色的，晚上吃了白色的。第二天我告诉医生，他问我：「你觉得怎么样？」我说：「我觉得很好啊。」他笑了：「那就对了，因为两种药是一样的。我只是想让你别忘了吃。」
text_pinyin: Wǒ shēngbìng le, yīshēng gěi le wǒ liǎng zhǒng yào. Báisè de zǎoshang chī, hóngsè de wǎnshang chī. Dànshì wǒ chī cuò le——zǎoshang chī le hóngsè de, wǎnshang chī le báisè de. Dì-èr tiān wǒ gàosu yīshēng, tā wèn wǒ:「Nǐ juéde zěnmeyàng?」Wǒ shuō:「Wǒ juéde hěn hǎo a.」Tā xiào le:「Nà jiù duì le, yīnwèi liǎng zhǒng yào shì yíyàng de. Wǒ zhǐ shì xiǎng ràng nǐ bié wàng le chī.」
text_en: I got sick and the doctor gave me two kinds of medicine. White ones in the morning, red ones at night. But I mixed them up — red in the morning, white at night. The next day I told the doctor. He asked: 'How do you feel?' I said: 'Pretty good, actually.' He laughed: 'That's right, because both medicines are the same. I just wanted to make sure you wouldn't forget to take them.'

questions.q_en: How many kinds of medicine did the doctor give?
What's different about the two medicines?

options.pinyin: yì zhǒng dà, yì zhǒng xiǎo
options.text_en: one is big, one is small

options.text: 颜色不一样，但其实一样
options.pinyin: yánsè bù yíyàng, dàn qíshí yíyàng
options.text_en: different colors but actually the same

options.pinyin: yì zhǒng guì, yì zhǒng piányi
options.text_en: one is expensive, one is cheap

questions.q_zh: 医生为什么给两种颜色的药？
questions.q_en: Why did the doctor give two colors of medicine?

options.pinyin: xiǎng ràng tā bié wàng le chī yào
options.text_en: to make sure he doesn't forget
because there were no white ones left

title: The Birthday Surprise
text_zh: 今天是我的生日。我的朋友们说要给我一个惊喜，让我晚上去他们家。我到了以后，门关着，灯也没开。我开了门，大声说：「我来了！」没有人回答。我找了半天，他们都不在家。后来他们打电话告诉我：「我们在你家等你呢！惊喜！」我笑了：「你们在我家，我在你们家，这是什么惊喜？」
text_pinyin: Jīntiān shì wǒ de shēngrì. Wǒ de péngyoumen shuō yào gěi wǒ yí gè jīngxǐ, ràng wǒ wǎnshang qù tāmen jiā. Wǒ dào le yǐhòu, mén guānzhe, dēng yě méi kāi. Wǒ kāi le mén, dàshēng shuō:「Wǒ lái le!」Méiyǒu rén huídá. Wǒ zhǎo le bàntiān, tāmen dōu bú zài jiā. Hòulái tāmen dǎ diànhuà gàosu wǒ:「Wǒmen zài nǐ jiā děng nǐ ne! Jīngxǐ!」Wǒ xiào le:「Nǐmen zài wǒ jiā, wǒ zài nǐmen jiā, zhè shì shénme jīngxǐ?」
text_en: Today is my birthday. My friends said they'd give me a surprise and told me to come to their place tonight. When I arrived, the door was shut and lights off. I opened the door and called out: 'I'm here!' No answer. I looked around forever — nobody home. Later they called: 'We're at YOUR place waiting for you! Surprise!' I laughed: 'You're at my place, I'm at yours — what kind of surprise is this?'

questions.q_en: Where did the friends tell 'I' to go?
Where are the friends?
How did the 'surprise' turn out?

options.text: 大家去了不一样的地方
options.pinyin: dàjiā qù le bù yíyàng de dìfang

title: Grandma's Egg Noodles
text_zh: 小时候，每次生日奶奶都给我做鸡蛋面条。她说：「面条长，所以吃面条的人也会长长地活着。」她做的面条很简单——只有鸡蛋、面条和一点点盐。但是比饭馆的好吃。现在奶奶不在了。我有时候自己做鸡蛋面条，但是怎么做都不是那个味道。也许不是面条的问题，是因为少了一个人。
text_pinyin: Xiǎo shíhou, měi cì shēngrì nǎinai dōu gěi wǒ zuò jīdàn miàntiáo. Tā shuō:「Miàntiáo cháng, suǒyǐ chī miàntiáo de rén yě huì cháng cháng de huózhe.」Tā zuò de miàntiáo hěn jiǎndān——zhǐ yǒu jīdàn, miàntiáo hé yìdiǎndiǎn yán. Dànshì bǐ fànguǎn de hǎochī. Xiànzài nǎinai bú zài le. Wǒ yǒu shíhou zìjǐ zuò jīdàn miàntiáo, dànshì zěnme zuò dōu búshì nàge wèidào. Yěxǔ búshì miàntiáo de wèntí, shì yīnwèi shǎo le yí gè rén.
text_en: When I was little, Grandma made me egg noodles for every birthday. She said: 'Noodles are long, so people who eat noodles will live a long life.' Her noodles were simple — just eggs, noodles, and a little salt. But better than any restaurant. Now Grandma is gone. Sometimes I make egg noodles myself, but no matter how I make them, they don't taste the same. Maybe it's not the noodles — maybe it's because someone is missing.

questions.q_zh: 奶奶什么时候做鸡蛋面条？
questions.q_en: When did Grandma make egg noodles?
What's in Grandma's noodles?

questions.q_zh: 现在「我」自己做的面条怎么样？
questions.q_en: How are the noodles 'I' makes now?

title: The Watermelon Test
text_zh: 我去买西瓜，旁边一个老人教我：「你要用手打一下西瓜。声音好听的，就是好的。」我打了一下——我觉得声音很好听。老人摇了摇头说：「不对，这个不好。」我又打了一个，他又说不好。我问他：「到底什么样的声音是好的？」他笑着说：「这个你要自己慢慢学。我学了三十年了。」最后他帮我选了一个。回家吃了，真的很好吃。
text_pinyin: Wǒ qù mǎi xīguā, pángbiān yí gè lǎorén jiāo wǒ:「Nǐ yào yòng shǒu dǎ yíxià xīguā. Shēngyīn hǎotīng de, jiù shì hǎo de.」Wǒ dǎ le yíxià——wǒ juéde shēngyīn hěn hǎotīng. Lǎorén yáo le yáo tóu shuō:「Búduì, zhège bù hǎo.」Wǒ yòu dǎ le yí gè, tā yòu shuō bù hǎo. Wǒ wèn tā:「Dàodǐ shénmeyàng de shēngyīn shì hǎo de?」Tā xiàozhe shuō:「Zhège nǐ yào zìjǐ mànmàn xué. Wǒ xué le sānshí nián le.」Zuìhòu tā bāng wǒ xuǎn le yí gè. Huí jiā chī le, zhēn de hěn hǎochī.
text_en: I go to buy watermelon. An old man next to me teaches me: 'Tap the watermelon with your hand. If it sounds good, it's a good one.' I tap one — I think it sounds good. The old man shakes his head: 'No, this one's not good.' I tap another; he says that's not good either. I ask: 'What exactly does a good one sound like?' He smiles: 'You have to learn this slowly on your own. I've been learning for thirty years.' In the end he picks one for me. I eat it at home — really delicious.

questions.q_en: How do you tell if a watermelon is good?
How long has the old man been learning?
Was the watermelon good in the end?
